per_turn_leaks.student_message: Eight to ten times a day is a dramatic increase. With that frequency, are the symptoms disrupting your sleep? Do you wake up at night needing to go?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I do wake up at night needing to go to the bathroom 2-3 times per night, and that's really affecting my sleep. I'm feeling exhausted all the time.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Nocturnal diarrhea is a red flag — it suggests an organic rather than functional cause. With that many bowel movements, do you feel like you're fully emptying each time, or is there a sensation of incomplete evacuation?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've been feeling like I'm not really finishing every bowel movement, even when I go multiple times in one day. It's this weird feeling of still needing to go after I've just gone.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Tenesmus with bloody diarrhea points strongly toward an inflammatory process in the rectum and colon. With this level of GI inflammation, the body often responds systemically. Have you had any fevers or chills?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I have had a low-grade fever a few times per week, usually around 37.8-38.0°C. It's been persistent and has me pretty concerned.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Low-grade fevers fit the pattern. Inflammatory bowel disease often has extraintestinal manifestations — the same inflammatory process can affect joints. Have you noticed any joint pain or stiffness?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah, I've had some achy pain in both knees and ankles for a while now, no swelling or redness to speak of. It's been pretty constant.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Knee and ankle pain — those are classic large joint manifestations of IBD. Are you taking anything for the joint pain? Any over-the-counter medications?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've been taking ibuprofen 2-3 times a week to help manage the joint pain, but it seems to make my abdominal symptoms worse, so I'm not sure if it's really helping.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Ibuprofen is important to note — NSAIDs can actually worsen inflammatory bowel disease. That might be contributing to your flare. With bloody diarrhea eight to ten times a day for three weeks, you must be losing weight. Have you noticed any changes on the scale?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah, I've lost about 4 kg (9 lbs) over the past three weeks, and my clothes are feeling looser too.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Four kilos in three weeks is significant — that's consistent with the severity of your symptoms. You mentioned you work long hours at a startup. That kind of stress can trigger and worsen GI inflammation. How are you coping with all of this?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've been trying to cope with all of this by drinking more beers than usual - like 4-5 nights a week now. It's become my way of unwinding after a long day.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Increased alcohol use as a coping mechanism — I understand, but alcohol can also irritate the GI tract and worsen inflammation. Let me ask about family history — inflammatory bowel disease has a strong genetic component. Has anyone in your family had Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis, or similar conditions?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease when she was 30 years old. It's been a few years now, and she's still managing it with medication.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Non-smoker, no drugs — that's good. Interestingly, ulcerative colitis is actually more common in non-smokers and ex-smokers.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've been trying to eat more bland foods like rice, bananas, and toast, but it's hard to keep anything down when I'm going to the bathroom so often. I've also tried taking probiotics and staying hydrated, but nothing seems to be making a big difference in my symptoms. Is there something specific you'd recommend or any advice on how to cope with this level of diarrhea?
